command.name: where
command.description: describe how command names are interpreted

options.description: for each name, print 'name: word', where 'word' is the kind of command
do a path search for the name, even if it's an alias/function/builtin
the arguments are taken as patterns (pattern characters must be quoted)
if the pathname contains symlinks, print the symlink-free name as well
print intermediate symlinks and the resolved name
expand tabs when outputting shell function
